articles.title: Delhi traffic police use tech helmets to protect officers in 43°C heatwave - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
New Delhi is experiencing a severe heatwave with temperatures reaching 43°C. In response, the Delhi Traffic Police have introduced innovative equipment, including air-conditioned helmets and portable fans, to help officers endure extreme conditions while managing heavy traffic.
The cooling helmets are currently being trialled among personnel who spend extended periods in direct sunlight. Assistant Sub-Inspector Veer Singh described the technology as transformative, stating, “This helmet keeps us relaxed in this weather. It can work easily for 4-5 hours, depending on the charge in its battery…”
The battery-powered cooling system provides effective relief for 4 to 5 hours, enabling officers to maintain comfort during peak heat periods. Additionally, mobile vans have been deployed to support officers managing VIP movements, serving as hydration and nutrition hubs to prevent exhaustion.
The initiative coincides with Red and Orange heat alerts issued by the India Meteorological Department across several states, indicating dangerously high temperatures.
Dr Randeep Guleria, Chairman of the Institute of Internal Medicine, Respiratory & Sleep Medicine at Medanta, warned of serious health risks associated with prolonged heat exposure. While minor issues such as prickly heat can be managed, he cautioned, “Prolonged exposure to extreme heat can trigger heat stroke, a potentially life-threatening condition in which the body loses its ability to regulate temperature.”
As the heatwave persists across North India, the Delhi Traffic Police’s approach highlights critical measures to protect frontline workers operating in hazardous conditions.

articles.title: No escape yet: Delhi baked under extreme heat, rain likely from June 11 - Mathrubhumi English
articles.full_text:
No escape yet: Delhi baked under extreme heat, rain likely from June 11
New Delhi: Delhi is expected to remain under intense heat conditions until Wednesday, with maximum temperatures likely to range between 42°C and 44°C, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
The capital continues to experience dry and oppressive weather conditions, with little to no rainfall, keeping temperatures significantly above normal levels.
Western disturbance to bring weather change from June 11
A fresh western disturbance is likely to affect northwest India from June 11, bringing a noticeable shift in Delhi’s weather pattern. Under its influence, the national capital is expected to witness thunderstorms, lightning activity, gusty winds and light rainfall on June 11 and 12.
The IMD has forecast wind speeds of 40–50 kmph, gusting up to 60 kmph during thunderstorm activity, which may provide temporary relief from the ongoing heat.
Sharp drop in temperatures expected
With the arrival of the weather system, Delhi is likely to see a significant drop in temperatures. Daytime temperatures are expected to fall by around 7–8°C by June 12, while minimum temperatures may reduce by 4–5°C.
By Thursday, maximum temperatures are forecast to ease into the range of 34°C to 36°C, marking a clear break from the current heatwave conditions.
Cloudy skies and gusty winds before rain
Ahead of the expected rainfall, Delhi is likely to witness partly cloudy skies on Tuesday and Wednesday. Surface winds will remain strong at around 20–30 kmph, gusting up to 40 kmph.
There is also a possibility of isolated thunderstorms during the afternoon or evening hours on Wednesday, signalling the approaching change in weather conditions.
Monsoon progress and regional outlook
While the southwest monsoon continues its gradual advancement over parts of central and eastern India, northwest India, including Delhi, is still awaiting its seasonal onset. However, IMD has indicated favourable conditions for further monsoon progress over the next few days.
Recent temperature readings highlight severity
On Monday, Delhi recorded a maximum temperature of 42.2°C at Safdarjung, which was 2.2°C above normal. The minimum temperature stood at 29.2°C.
Other parts of the city reported even higher readings, with Ridge recording 43.4°C, Lodhi Road at 42.6°C and Palam at 41.8°C, highlighting the intensity of the ongoing heat conditions.

articles.title: 46°C on the horizon? Delhi’s week-long heat forecast decoded - Dynamite News
articles.full_text:
New Delhi: Delhi is set to experience persistent extreme heat this week, with daytime temperatures forecast to range from 43°C to 46°C. Officials warn residents to take precautions as prolonged exposure to high temperatures could increase heat-related illnesses.
The seven-day forecast predicts a peak of 46°C on Wednesday, followed by 45°C on Tuesday and Thursday, 44°C on Sunday, and 43°C on Friday, Saturday, and Monday. Nighttime lows will remain unusually high, with the minimum near 37°C, offering little overnight relief.
On Tuesday, the mercury is expected to hover around 37°C for much of the day, while the maximum could reach 45°C. Winds are forecast from the west-northwest at about 12.4 km/h, with gusts up to 16.3 km/h, potentially increasing perceived heat through dust and reduced air quality.
Health authorities advise limiting outdoor activity during peak afternoon hours, staying hydrated, wearing lightweight clothing, and checking on vulnerable groups such as the elderly and young children. Employers are urged to adapt working hours for outdoor laborers to reduce heat exposure.
Municipal services are preparing cooling shelters and increasing outreach to vulnerable neighborhoods. Citizens are encouraged to follow advisories from local health departments and meteorological agencies for updates.
Residents should monitor official weather updates and heed heat warnings as the city navigates several days of intense heat.
Location : New Delhi
Published : 9 June 2026, 9:06 AM IST
Topics : Delhi heatwave Delhi Temperature Delhi Weather Forecast Weather Update weekly weather Delhi

articles.title: देशभर में मौसम का डबल अटैक, कहीं लू तो कहीं बारिश; दिल्ली में 42°C का कहर, बंगाल में मानसून की दस्तक - samarsaleel.com
articles.full_text:
नई दिल्ली, (समर सलिल)। देश के अलग-अलग हिस्सों में इस समय मौसम का मिला-जुला असर देखने को मिल रहा है। कहीं भीषण गर्मी और लू लोगों को परेशान कर रही है, तो कहीं तेज बारिश और मानसून की एंट्री ने मौसम का रुख बदल दिया है।
आंध्र प्रदेश में लू और बारिश दोनों का असर
Andhra Pradesh में मौसम का दोहरा रूप देखने को मिल रहा है। कुछ जिलों में अगले दो दिनों तक गरज-चमक के साथ हल्की से मध्यम बारिश की संभावना जताई गई है, जबकि कई इलाकों में भीषण लू का असर जारी रहेगा। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार कई मंडलों में तापमान 42 से 44 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच सकता है, जिससे गर्मी का प्रकोप और बढ़ गया है। वहीं कुछ क्षेत्रों में बादलों और कम दबाव की स्थिति के कारण बारिश भी हो सकती है।
दिल्ली में 42 डिग्री तक पहुंचा पारा
राष्ट्रीय राजधानी Delhi में सुबह से ही गर्म मौसम बना रहा। अधिकतम तापमान करीब 42 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंचने का अनुमान है। कुछ इलाकों में हल्की बारिश जरूर दर्ज की गई, लेकिन उमस और गर्मी से राहत नहीं मिली। साथ ही वायु गुणवत्ता मध्यम श्रेणी में बनी हुई है, जिससे लोगों को गर्मी के साथ प्रदूषण की भी दोहरी मार झेलनी पड़ रही है।
उत्तर बंगाल में मानसून की एंट्री
West Bengal के उत्तर बंगाल क्षेत्र में मानसून के प्रवेश की परिस्थितियां अनुकूल बन रही हैं। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार आने वाले दिनों में दार्जिलिंग, जलपाईगुड़ी और आसपास के क्षेत्रों में भारी बारिश हो सकती है। कई जगहों पर पहले ही तेज बारिश दर्ज की जा चुकी है, जिससे मौसम में ठंडक बढ़ी है और मानसून की औपचारिक दस्तक के संकेत मिल रहे हैं।
मौसम विभाग की चेतावनी और सलाह
मौसम विभाग ने लोगों को सावधानी बरतने की सलाह दी है। खासकर गर्मी और लू वाले क्षेत्रों में:
- बच्चों, बुजुर्गों और बीमार लोगों का विशेष ध्यान रखें
- तेज बारिश और आंधी के दौरान सुरक्षित स्थान पर रहें
देशभर में बदलता मौसम
फिलहाल देश के कई हिस्सों में मौसम का यह असंतुलित पैटर्न जारी है-कहीं लू से लोग परेशान हैं तो कहीं बारिश से राहत मिल रही है। आने वाले दिनों में मौसम में और बदलाव देखने की संभावना जताई जा रही है।
